name: wallet-selection-hl-survivor
description: SURVIVOR / TAIL-SAFETY selection + rating config for Hyperliquid perp traders. 'Best' = the trader LEAST LIKELY TO BLOW UP, not the highest return. Primary screen = capital preservation: bounded drawdown, no liquidations, stop discipline (cuts losers rather than ride-or-liquidate), bounded worst-loss vs median-win, contained max adverse excursion. Win rate is treated as a RED FLAG above 95% (a no-stop vol-seller / martingale manufactures a near-perfect record by warehousing unrealized losses — the trap every June-2026 copy study hit). Adapted from wallet-selection-v0.1.0 (Jupiter) to HL-available/computable metrics: round-trip closedPnl from cached fills, portfolio accountValue/pnl history, and the persistence-study diagnostics (sharpe, maxDrawdownFrac, studyReturn). DECISION-SUPPORT for MANUAL review only — HL copy-trading was rejected 5 ways; this surfaces the rare genuinely-survivable accounts, it does not promise profit.
changelog: 0.1.0 — initial HL survivor lens. Tuned to: (A) per-account round-trip stats computed from cached fills (closedPnl, dir, fee, coin) — roundTripWinRate, profitFactor, worstLossVsMedianWin, maxConsecLossUsd, holdTime, stopRate; (B) portfolio-derived equity drawdown (maxDrawdownFromPeakPct, longestDrawdownDays); (C) persistence diagnostics (sharpeAnnual, maxDrawdownFrac, studyPeriodReturn, best5StepsShare). Liquidations inferred from fill dir/markPx. Validation intent: an account with >95% round-trip win rate AND zero realized stops AND a single fat worst-loss should grade C-D + EXTREME_WIN_RATE/NO_STOPS flags; an account with a deep equity drawdown (>60% from peak) or an inferred liquidation should AUTO-DISQUALIFY.

eligibility._doc: Hard cutoffs to even be scored. Below these = excluded (not graded F, just not enough signal). HL accounts are noisy and short-lived; require real history and enough round-trips that the tail had a chance to show.
eligibility._fallbackWhenNoFills: Accounts with portfolio + persistence diagnostics but no cached fills can be PROVISIONALLY graded on equity-drawdown + sharpe + studyReturn only; flagged PROVISIONAL_NO_FILLS and capped at grade B (cannot earn A without round-trip tail evidence).

riskDiscipline._doc: PRIMARY criteria. These bound the tail — the whole point of the survivor lens. A blow-up risk lives here, not in the headline return.

maxDrawdownFrac._doc: PRIMARY survivor metric. Return-based max drawdown from the persistence study (persistent-set.json maxDrawdownFrac) — the deepest equity erosion measured on RETURNS, not raw account value, so it is NOT corrupted by deposits/withdrawals. Scale runs ~0.28 (smoothest) to ~6.0 (boom-bust) across the HL persistent set. A frac >1 means the worst stretch erased more than a full normalization base of equity = the boom-bust pattern the survivor lens rejects. Bands tuned to the observed HL distribution.

rawEquityDrawdownFromPeakPct._doc: SECONDARY / SOFT signal only. Peak-to-trough drawdown from raw portfolio accountValueHistory. KNOWN-NOISY: the cache has no deposit/withdraw flow data, so an account that grew from a near-zero starting balance shows a spurious ~1.0 drawdown. Used as a tie-breaker and a soft flag (DEEP_DRAWDOWN) only — NOT a hard disqualifier. The return-based maxDrawdownFrac is the trustworthy one.

worstLossVsMedianWin._doc: Largest single realized round-trip LOSS as a multiple of the median round-trip WIN. THE blow-up flag for the survivor lens. A martingale/vol-seller shows many small wins and one catastrophic loss => huge ratio. Bounded worst-loss is the core ask.

stopRate._doc: Fraction of LOSING round-trips that were realized as a contained loss (cut) vs the account showing a ride-or-liquidate pattern (rare giant losses, no small ones). Proxy from fills: share of losing round-trips whose loss is <= 2x the median win (i.e. the trader takes the small loss). High = disciplined cutter; low = warehouses losses then dumps. 0 = pure ride-or-liquidate.

maxAdverseExcursionPct._doc: Worst within-window equity excursion below the prior peak as a fraction of that peak, from accountValueHistory between round-trip closes. How deep underwater the account sat before recovering (warehoused unrealized risk).

longestDrawdownDays._doc: Longest stretch (days) the account spent below a prior equity peak. A long underwater stretch = a position being ridden, the survivor anti-pattern.

liquidations._doc: Realized liquidations in the covered window, inferred from fills (dir contains 'Liquidat', or a Close at a price gapped far through the position vs prior marks). Any liquidation is a tail that ALREADY FIRED — the exact event the survivor lens exists to avoid.

sharpeFloor._doc: Annualized Sharpe from the persistence study (return per unit of volatility). A survivor wants smooth equity; negative/low Sharpe means the account is volatile relative to what it earns. Floor only — high Sharpe is good but not the goal (low DD matters more).

openExposureGuard._doc: Live open exposure guard (SOFT — same raw-equity noise caveat as rawEquityDrawdownFromPeakPct, so it raises a flag, it does NOT hard-reject in v0.1.0). Flags accounts whose most recent equity sits deeply below their own recent peak (a position possibly being ridden underwater right now). Upgrade to a hard reject once deposit/withdraw flow data is available to clean the signal.

performance._doc: SECONDARY. Necessary but not sufficient — and win rate is BOUNDED ON BOTH ENDS, not maximized. The survivor lens explicitly PENALIZES extreme win rate.

roundTripWinRate._doc: Round-trip win rate from closedPnl. floor = must clear noise to be real. healthyMax = above this is fine but no extra credit. suspiciousAbove = 0.95 => EXTREME_WIN_RATE flag + penalty: a near-perfect record on a perp book is the vol-seller/martingale signature, the single most reliable predictor of an eventual blow-up.

profitFactorBands._doc: Letter→grade-points (4/3/2/0) cutoffs for profit factor inside the profitability category. gradeA/gradeB are above minProfitFactor; below minProfitFactor scores 0.

studyPeriodReturnBands._doc: Grade-points cutoffs (4/3/2/1/0) for study-period return inside profitability.

maxBest5StepsShare._doc: Share of total study P&L concentrated in the best 5 forward steps (from persistence diagnostics). >1.0 means a few windows carried everything and the rest bled — fragile, not survivable. Penalize concentration.

ratingRubric._doc: Per-category letter grades A-F (A=4..F=0). Overall = weighted average, but ANY autoDisqualifier floors overall to F. Weights tilt HARD toward drawdown/tail-safety (survivor lens): capitalPreservation + tailSafety = 60% of the grade.

consistency._doc: Fraction of the 5-6 forward study windows with positive return (windowReturns from persistence diagnostics). Survivor wants to grind across regimes, not boom-bust.

copyabilityForSurvivor._doc: Practical follower risk under THIS lens: a survivor account is copyable if a follower mirroring it would never have faced a margin event. Reserve = idle capital (as a multiple of typical position) needed to survive the worst observed adverse excursion without liquidation. Lower = safer to follow.

flagThresholds._doc: Soft-flag cutoffs (not disqualifiers), lifted from the scorer so they are config-driven. FAT_WORST_LOSS: worstLossVsMedianWin above worstLossFlagAbove (the tailSafety gradeC bound); RIDE_OR_LIQUIDATE: stopRate at/below rideOrLiquidateStopRateAtOrBelow; NO_STOPS: stopRate == 0 (exact).

autoDisqualifiers._doc: Any TRUE => overall grade F + 'DISQUALIFIED'. These encode the survivor lessons: a fired tail or a deep equity hole is disqualifying no matter how pretty the return.

thresholds._doc: Machine-readable disqualifier cutoffs the scorer evaluates (JSON is the source of truth). maxDrawdownFracAbove/worstLossVsMedianWinAbove/sharpeAnnualBelow mirror the riskDiscipline hardRejects; the compound stopRate==0 + winRate>0.95 rules use noStopWorstLossAbove and extremeWinRateAbove.

overall._doc: Emit BOTH a letter and a 0-10 score per category and overall. A disqualified wallet shows F/0 with breached rule(s). Provisional (no-fills) accounts capped at B.

uiHints._doc: For the trader-browser UI: columns/badges so a human can scan and select for MANUAL follow review.
